value.found_quote: Remote connections let you use Codex from another device or another machine... Start new threads in projects on the host, or continue existing ones. Send follow-up instructions, answer questions, and steer active work. Approve commands and other actions. Review outputs, diffs, test results, terminal output, and screenshots. Get notified when Codex completes a task or needs your attention. Switch between connected hosts and threads.
value.rationale: The fetched page is entirely dedicated to OpenAI's "Remote connections" feature for Codex. It explicitly describes the capability to use Codex from another device or machine, and the bullet-point list under "What you can do remotely" directly matches every element of the claim: starting/continuing threads, steering work (send follow-up instructions), approving actions (approve commands and other actions), reviewing outputs, and receiving notifications (get notified when Codex completes a task or needs your attention). The page presents this as a current, fully documented feature with no mention of preview, beta, or deprecation status, indicating it is generally available/active.
